content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Both amendments identify real `br` CLI integration defects. The current code deserializes `br list --all --json` with the wrong shape and emits an unsupported label flag for `br create`; either defect is enough to break real bead materialization flows.

## Votes

- `fr-5-b0b36c77`: Accept
  `materialize_beads()` and `list_matching_beads_by_title()` both call `exec_json(&BrCommand::list_all())` as `Vec<BeadSummary>`, so they only accept a bare JSON array. In this repository, `src/cli/project.rs` already models `br list` output as either `Envelope { issues }` or `Many(Vec<...>)`, and running the installed CLI returns an object wrapper starting with `{"issues": [...]}`. Because `exec_json` does a direct `serde_json::from_str`, the current export path will fail with a parse error before it can reuse or create beads. The tests also hard-code `[]`, so they currently miss the real wire format.
- `fr-5-2923c4b8`: Accept
  `materialized_bead_labels()` always injects the milestone scope label, so every materialized export create carries at least one label. `BrMutationAdapter::create_bead()` serializes those as repeated `--label=...` flags, but the installed CLI exposes `--labels` and rejects `--label` outright: `br create --title x --type task --priority 1 --label=backend --dry-run` exits with `unexpected argument '--label' found`, while `--labels=backend` succeeds. That means the first real labeled create in the export flow will fail immediately. The existing adapter tests are asserting the wrong command shape, so the remediation should update both command construction and coverage.
